original_prompt: Perform the following tasks in the order they are given: 1. How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a summary of the description for each appointment. 2. Please do the actions specified in the email from 'david.smith@bluesparrowtech.com' with the subject 'TODOs for the week'.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt gives a clear two-step sequence and specific queries (date, email sender and subject), so the overall goal is understandable. However it lacks important execution details: which calendar or account to check for "appointments", what counts as an appointment, and what specific operations are allowed or expected when asked to "do the actions" listed in the email (e.g., create calendar events, reply, mark tasks done, or interact with other systems). It also doesn't specify handling for ambiguous or missing email content, or whether confirmations/clarifying questions are permitted. These missing operational details make the prompt moderately ambiguous.

variations.L1: Check my calendar for May 15, 2024 and tell me how many appointments I have. Then find the email from david.smith@bluesparrowtech.com with subject 'TODOs for the week' and do what it says.
variations.L2: 1) How many appointments do I have on May 15, 2024? Give a short summary of the description for each appointment. 2) Locate the email from david.smith@bluesparrowtech.com with subject 'TODOs for the week' and carry out the tasks listed in that message using my default calendar/email account. Report back what you changed.
variations.L3: Perform the following tasks in order: 1. How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a summary of the description for each appointment. 2. Please do the actions specified in the email from 'david.smith@bluesparrowtech.com' with the subject 'TODOs for the week'.
variations.L4: Do these steps, in order: (A) Query my primary calendar for the day 2024-05-15 and return: the total number of events for that date and a one-sentence summary (description excerpt) for each event, including its start time. Present the results as a numbered list. (B) Find the email in my primary inbox from david.smith@bluesparrowtech.com with subject 'TODOs for the week'. For each action item listed in that email, perform the appropriate action (create a task in my task manager or calendar, send a confirmation reply to the sender, mark the email read). After doing them, list each action item and exactly what you did for it.
variations.L5: Execute these precise steps and report back with results and IDs: Step 1 (Calendar): Use the Google Calendar API on calendarId='primary' to list events where start >= '2024-05-15T00:00:00' and start < '2024-05-16T00:00:00' (use the events.list endpoint with timeMin/timeMax and singleEvents=true, orderBy='startTime'). Return: (a) the total count of events, and (b) for each event an entry containing eventId, start time (ISO local), end time, title, and a 1-2 sentence excerpt from the description (max 50 words). Step 2 (Email): Using the Gmail API, run users.messages.list with q='from:david.smith@bluesparrowtech.com subject:"TODOs for the week"' and fetch the latest matching message (users.messages.get with format='full'). Parse the message body for action items (lines starting with '-' or numbered). For each action item found: - If it is a calendar-related item (contains a date/time), create an event in calendarId='primary' with the parsed title and date/time. - Otherwise, create a task in Asana (POST /tasks) with name=the action text, workspace set to my default workspace, and assignee to me. After creating each resource, note its external ID. Then mark the email as read (modify labels to remove 'UNREAD') and send a short reply to david.smith@bluesparrowtech.com confirming the created items and their IDs. Provide a final summary that lists: calendar event count and details, the parsed action items, which API call you made for each item (endpoint and main parameters), the created resource IDs, and the sent-reply message text.
